round63b_candidates.name: r63b_combo_floor_off_r62soft
round63b_candidates.description: Exact Round62 winner plus the Round63-A floor-off rule: below 0.08x disable the iter2 once-per-phase SCD entirely.
round63b_candidates.rationale: Direct combination baseline. Tests whether Round62’s ultra-low alpha-guard is already enough to absorb the SCD-off tradeoff.

round63b_candidates.name: r63b_combo_floor_off_003only
round63b_candidates.description: Keep the Round62 winner intact through 0.05x, and only at 0.03x disable the iter2 once-per-phase SCD.
round63b_candidates.rationale: Weaker floor-off variant. If 0.05x is still in the sweet spot, this localizes the SCD ablation to the deepest ultra-low regime only.

round63b_candidates.name: r63b_combo_alpha09995_r62soft
round63b_candidates.description: Round62 winner plus a milder ultra-low SCD alpha: below 0.08x change once-per-phase SCD alpha from 0.999 to 0.9995.
round63b_candidates.rationale: Alpha-weaken variant from Round63-A. Keeps the SCD body alive, but softens the cut enough that Round62 guard may handle the remaining ultra-low overshoot.

round63b_candidates.name: r63b_combo_core3_r62soft
round63b_candidates.description: Round62 winner plus a smaller ultra-low SCD target: below 0.08x keep SCD only on the Round61 backbone trio dKg_xx / dKg_xy / dKg_zz.
round63b_candidates.rationale: Target-weaken variant from Round63-A. Keeps the SCD idea only on the three states that still looked structurally helpful, while Round62 guard handles yy / Ka_xx-side overshoot.
